title: Box<WOLFTPM2_DEV> for stable C pointer
description: Device and WolfTpmLinuxDev heap-allocate WOLFTPM2_DEV via Box rather than storing it inline. This gives the C wolfTPM2_* functions a stable memory address for the duration of the device's lifetime. If stored inline in a Rust struct, Rust could move the struct on assignment or vector reallocation, leaving the C library with a dangling pointer. The Box is also the mechanism that makes unsafe impl Send for Device safe: no thread-local state and a fixed address. Alternative rejected: Pin<Box<...>> was unnecessary since no self-referential Rust structs are involved; plain Box is sufficient.
